---
title: "Architecture, Abstraction, and Design"
---

# Architecture, Abstraction, and Design

Range: `0001-0112`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 112   |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ----- | ----------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0001  | [abstraction](#rune-0001)           | authored   | A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism. Shadow: hiding the only failure that matters.                                                                      |
| 0002  | [adapter](#rune-0002)               | authored   | A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another. Shadow: adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it.                                       |
| 0003  | [agent {Arch}](#rune-0003)          | authored   | `agent` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the agent obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0004  | [aggregate {Arch}](#rune-0004)      | authored   | `aggregate` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the aggregate obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0005  | [anti-corruption layer](#rune-0005) | authored   | A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.                                                                                                                                                                |
| 0006  | [API gateway](#rune-0006)           | authored   | A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world. Shadow: a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.                                                                     |
| 0007  | [architecture](#rune-0007)          | authored   | `architecture` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the architecture obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0008  | [artifact boundary](#rune-0008)     | authored   | `artifact boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the artifact boundary obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 0009  | [assembler](#rune-0009)             | authored   | `assembler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the assembler obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0010  | [backbone](#rune-0010)              | authored   | `backbone` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backbone obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0011  | [backend](#rune-0011)               | authored   | `backend` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backend obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0012  | [boundary](#rune-0012)              | authored   | The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins. Shadow: porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.                                                                                        |
| 0013  | [bounded context](#rune-0013)       | authored   | A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem. Shadow: false unification through shared names.                                                                            |
| 0014  | [bridge](#rune-0014)                | authored   | `bridge` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the bridge obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 0015  | [broker {Arch}](#rune-0015)         | authored   | `broker` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the broker obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0016  | [builder](#rune-0016)               | authored   | `builder` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the builder obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0017  | [bus {Arch}](#rune-0017)            | authored   | `bus` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the bus obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0018  | [capability {Arch}](#rune-0018)     | authored   | `capability` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the capability obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 0019  | [channel](#rune-0019)               | authored   | `channel` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the channel obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0020  | [client {Arch}](#rune-0020)         | authored   | `client` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the client obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0021  | [cluster {Arch}](#rune-0021)        | authored   | `cluster` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the cluster obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 0022  | [command bus](#rune-0022)           | authored   | `command bus` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the command bus obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 0023  | [component {Arch}](#rune-0023)      | authored   | A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 0024  | [composition](#rune-0024)           | authored   | `composition` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the composition obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 0025  | [connector](#rune-0025)             | authored   | `connector` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the connector obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0026  | [container {Arch}](#rune-0026)      | authored   | `container` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the container obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0027  | [control plane {Arch}](#rune-0027)  | authored   | `control plane` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the control plane obligation named before work proceeds.   |
| 0028  | [coordinator](#rune-0028)           | authored   | `coordinator` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the coordinator obligation named before work proceeds.                         |
| 0029  | [core {Arch}](#rune-0029)           | authored   | `core` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the core obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 0030  | [coupling](#rune-0030)              | authored   | The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.                                                                                   |
| 0031  | [daemon {Arch}](#rune-0031)         | authored   | `daemon` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the daemon obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0032  | [data plane {Arch}](#rune-0032)     | authored   | `data plane` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the data plane obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 0033  | [decorator {Arch}](#rune-0033)      | authored   | `decorator` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the decorator obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0034  | [dependency inversion](#rune-0034)  | authored   | Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail. Shadow: concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.                                                                                      |
| 0035  | [dispatcher](#rune-0035)            | authored   | `dispatcher` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the dispatcher obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 0036  | [domain model](#rune-0036)          | authored   | The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.                                                                                                                                                     |
| 0037  | [edge service](#rune-0037)          | authored   | `edge service` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the edge service obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0038  | [engine](#rune-0038)                | authored   | `engine` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the engine obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 0039  | [entrypoint](#rune-0039)            | authored   | `entrypoint` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the entrypoint obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 0040  | [event bus](#rune-0040)             | authored   | `event bus` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the event bus obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0041  | [event handler](#rune-0041)         | authored   | `event handler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the event handler obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 0042  | [executor](#rune-0042)              | authored   | `executor` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the executor obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0043  | [facade](#rune-0043)                | authored   | A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 0044  | [factory](#rune-0044)               | authored   | `factory` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the factory obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0045  | [feature module](#rune-0045)        | authored   | `feature module` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the feature module obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0046  | [frontend](#rune-0046)              | authored   | `frontend` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the frontend obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0047  | [gateway {Arch}](#rune-0047)        | authored   | `gateway` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the gateway obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 0048  | [graph](#rune-0048)                 | authored   | `graph` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the graph obligation named before work proceeds.                                     |
| 0049  | [handler](#rune-0049)               | authored   | `handler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the handler obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0050  | [hierarchy {Arch}](#rune-0050)      | authored   | `hierarchy` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hierarchy obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0051  | [hook](#rune-0051)                  | authored   | `hook` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hook obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 0052  | [host](#rune-0052)                  | authored   | `host` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the host obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 0053  | [hub](#rune-0053)                   | authored   | `hub` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hub obligation named before work proceeds.                                         |
| 0054  | [implementation {Arch}](#rune-0054) | authored   | `implementation` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the implementation obligation named before work proceeds. |
| 0055  | [interface {Arch}](#rune-0055)      | authored   | A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs. Shadow: tight coupling and type bleed.                                                                                                 |
| 0056  | [interpreter {Arch}](#rune-0056)    | authored   | `interpreter` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the interpreter obligation named before work proceeds.       |
| 0057  | [kernel service](#rune-0057)        | authored   | `kernel service` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the kernel service obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0058  | [layer](#rune-0058)                 | authored   | A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 0059  | [library](#rune-0059)               | authored   | `library` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the library obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0060  | [listener](#rune-0060)              | authored   | `listener` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the listener obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0061  | [machine boundary](#rune-0061)      | authored   | `machine boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the machine boundary obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 0062  | [mediator](#rune-0062)              | authored   | `mediator` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the mediator obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0063  | [mesh {Arch}](#rune-0063)           | authored   | `mesh` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the mesh obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 0064  | [microservice](#rune-0064)          | authored   | `microservice` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the microservice obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0065  | [middleware](#rune-0065)            | authored   | Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.                                                                                                                                                           |
| 0066  | [module {Arch}](#rune-0066)         | authored   | A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.                                                                                                                                                                |
| 0067  | [monolith](#rune-0067)              | authored   | `monolith` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the monolith obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0068  | [namespace {Arch}](#rune-0068)      | authored   | `namespace` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the namespace obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 0069  | [node {Arch}](#rune-0069)           | authored   | `node` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the node obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 0070  | [observer](#rune-0070)              | authored   | `observer` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the observer obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0071  | [orchestrator](#rune-0071)          | authored   | A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.                                                                                                                                                |
| 0072  | [package {Arch}](#rune-0072)        | authored   | `package` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the package obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 0073  | [pipeline {Arch}](#rune-0073)       | authored   | `pipeline` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the pipeline obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 0074  | [platform](#rune-0074)              | authored   | `platform` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the platform obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0075  | [plugin](#rune-0075)                | authored   | An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.                                                                                                                                                  |
| 0076  | [port {Arch}](#rune-0076)           | authored   | `port` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the port obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 0077  | [presenter](#rune-0077)             | authored   | `presenter` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the presenter obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0078  | [process manager](#rune-0078)       | authored   | `process manager` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the process manager obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0079  | [producer](#rune-0079)              | authored   | `producer` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the producer obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0080  | [projection](#rune-0080)            | authored   | `projection` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the projection obligation named before work proceeds.                           |
| 0081  | [protocol boundary](#rune-0081)     | authored   | `protocol boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the protocol boundary obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 0082  | [proxy {Arch}](#rune-0082)          | authored   | A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 0083  | [queue {Arch}](#rune-0083)          | authored   | `queue` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the queue obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0084  | [reactor](#rune-0084)               | authored   | `reactor` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the reactor obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0085  | [receiver](#rune-0085)              | authored   | `receiver` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the receiver obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0086  | [registry](#rune-0086)              | authored   | `registry` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the registry obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0087  | [repository {Arch}](#rune-0087)     | authored   | `repository` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the repository obligation named before work proceeds.         |
| 0088  | [resource boundary](#rune-0088)     | authored   | `resource boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the resource boundary obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 0089  | [router {Arch}](#rune-0089)         | authored   | `router` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the router obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0090  | [scheduler](#rune-0090)             | authored   | `scheduler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the scheduler obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0091  | [seam](#rune-0091)                  | authored   | `seam` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the seam obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 0092  | [service](#rune-0092)               | authored   | A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.                                                                                                                                                                       |
| 0093  | [service mesh {Arch}](#rune-0093)   | authored   | `service mesh` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the service mesh obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 0094  | [session boundary](#rune-0094)      | authored   | `session boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the session boundary obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 0095  | [shard {Arch}](#rune-0095)          | authored   | `shard` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the shard obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0096  | [shim](#rune-0096)                  | authored   | `shim` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the shim obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 0097  | [singleton](#rune-0097)             | authored   | `singleton` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the singleton obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0098  | [source of truth](#rune-0098)       | authored   | The place other copies are supposed to agree with.                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 0099  | [stack {Arch}](#rune-0099)          | authored   | `stack` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the stack obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0100  | [stage {Arch}](#rune-0100)          | authored   | `stage` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the stage obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 0101  | [state machine](#rune-0101)         | authored   | Behavior expressed as named states and legal transitions.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 0102  | [strategy](#rune-0102)              | authored   | `strategy` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the strategy obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0103  | [subsystem](#rune-0103)             | authored   | `subsystem` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the subsystem obligation named before work proceeds.                             |
| 0104  | [surface](#rune-0104)               | authored   | `surface` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the surface obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 0105  | [system](#rune-0105)                | authored   | `system` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the system obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 0106  | [template method](#rune-0106)       | authored   | `template method` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the template method obligation named before work proceeds.                 |
| 0107  | [tree](#rune-0107)                  | authored   | `tree` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the tree obligation named before work proceeds.                                       |
| 0108  | [use case](#rune-0108)              | authored   | `use case` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the use case obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0109  | [value object](#rune-0109)          | authored   | `value object` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the value object obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 0110  | [worker](#rune-0110)                | authored   | `worker` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the worker obligation named before work proceeds.                                   |
| 0111  | [workflow](#rune-0111)              | authored   | `workflow` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the workflow obligation named before work proceeds.                               |
| 0112  | [wrapper](#rune-0112)               | authored   | `wrapper` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the wrapper obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |

## Entries

### [0001] abstraction {#rune-0001}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about without drowning in mechanism.

**Shadow:** hiding the only failure that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Deliberate forgetting of detail so larger structure can be reasoned about cleanly.

### [0002] adapter {#rune-0002}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A negotiated translator between unlike shapes. Use it when two systems must meet without infecting one another.

**Shadow:** adapter jungles that preserve confusion rather than resolve it.

**Pocket gloss:** A translator between unlike shapes that lets systems meet without infecting one another.

### [0003] agent {Arch} {#rune-0003}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `agent` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the agent obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `agent` at rune 0003 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0004] aggregate {Arch} {#rune-0004}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `aggregate` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the aggregate obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `aggregate` at rune 0004 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0005] anti-corruption layer {#rune-0005}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

**Shadow:** misusing `anti-corruption layer` at rune 0005 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A protective seam that keeps one model from polluting another.

### [0006] API gateway {#rune-0006}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing at the edge of a service world.

**Shadow:** a convenience chokepoint that quietly becomes a monolith.

**Pocket gloss:** An edge threshold that centralizes entry, policy, and routing.

### [0007] architecture {#rune-0007}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `architecture` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the architecture obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `architecture` at rune 0007 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0008] artifact boundary {#rune-0008}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `artifact boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the artifact boundary obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `artifact boundary` at rune 0008 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0009] assembler {#rune-0009}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `assembler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the assembler obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `assembler` at rune 0009 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0010] backbone {#rune-0010}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `backbone` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backbone obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `backbone` at rune 0010 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0011] backend {#rune-0011}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `backend` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the backend obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `backend` at rune 0011 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0012] boundary {#rune-0012}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The line that says where one responsibility ends and another begins.

**Shadow:** porous edges that smear blame, ownership, and test scope.

**Pocket gloss:** The line where one responsibility ends and another begins.

### [0013] bounded context {#rune-0013}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A protection against semantic bleed: the same word may not mean the same thing in every subsystem.

**Shadow:** false unification through shared names.

**Pocket gloss:** A semantic fence: the same term is not forced to mean the same thing everywhere.

### [0014] bridge {#rune-0014}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `bridge` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the bridge obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `bridge` at rune 0014 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0015] broker {Arch} {#rune-0015}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `broker` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the broker obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `broker` at rune 0015 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0016] builder {#rune-0016}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `builder` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the builder obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `builder` at rune 0016 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0017] bus {Arch} {#rune-0017}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `bus` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the bus obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `bus` at rune 0017 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0018] capability {Arch} {#rune-0018}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `capability` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the capability obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `capability` at rune 0018 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0019] channel {#rune-0019}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `channel` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the channel obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `channel` at rune 0019 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0020] client {Arch} {#rune-0020}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `client` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the client obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `client` at rune 0020 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0021] cluster {Arch} {#rune-0021}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `cluster` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the cluster obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `cluster` at rune 0021 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0022] command bus {#rune-0022}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `command bus` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the command bus obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `command bus` at rune 0022 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0023] component {Arch} {#rune-0023}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.

**Shadow:** misusing `component` at rune 0023 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A bounded unit of responsibility inside a larger design.

### [0024] composition {#rune-0024}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `composition` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the composition obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `composition` at rune 0024 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0025] connector {#rune-0025}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `connector` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the connector obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `connector` at rune 0025 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0026] container {Arch} {#rune-0026}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `container` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the container obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `container` at rune 0026 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0027] control plane {Arch} {#rune-0027}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `control plane` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the control plane obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `control plane` at rune 0027 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0028] coordinator {#rune-0028}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `coordinator` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the coordinator obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `coordinator` at rune 0028 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0029] core {Arch} {#rune-0029}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `core` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the core obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `core` at rune 0029 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0030] coupling {#rune-0030}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere. Name it early if you want modularity to survive contact with reality.

**Shadow:** when a change in one component quietly forces coordinated edits across unrelated surfaces.

**Pocket gloss:** The hidden tax paid when change in one place forces change elsewhere.

### [0031] daemon {Arch} {#rune-0031}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `daemon` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the daemon obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `daemon` at rune 0031 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0032] data plane {Arch} {#rune-0032}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `data plane` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the data plane obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `data plane` at rune 0032 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0033] decorator {Arch} {#rune-0033}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `decorator` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the decorator obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `decorator` at rune 0033 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0034] dependency inversion {#rune-0034}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail.

**Shadow:** concrete dependencies hard-wired into places that should remain portable.

**Pocket gloss:** Stable policy should not kneel before unstable detail.

### [0035] dispatcher {#rune-0035}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `dispatcher` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the dispatcher obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `dispatcher` at rune 0035 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0036] domain model {#rune-0036}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.

**Shadow:** misusing `domain model` at rune 0036 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** The conceptual map of the business world the software is trying to serve.

### [0037] edge service {#rune-0037}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `edge service` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the edge service obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `edge service` at rune 0037 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0038] engine {#rune-0038}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `engine` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the engine obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `engine` at rune 0038 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0039] entrypoint {#rune-0039}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `entrypoint` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the entrypoint obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `entrypoint` at rune 0039 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0040] event bus {#rune-0040}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `event bus` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the event bus obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `event bus` at rune 0040 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0041] event handler {#rune-0041}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `event handler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the event handler obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `event handler` at rune 0041 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0042] executor {#rune-0042}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `executor` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the executor obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `executor` at rune 0042 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0043] facade {#rune-0043}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.

**Shadow:** misusing `facade` at rune 0043 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A simplified front door hiding a messier interior.

### [0044] factory {#rune-0044}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `factory` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the factory obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `factory` at rune 0044 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0045] feature module {#rune-0045}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `feature module` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the feature module obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `feature module` at rune 0045 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0046] frontend {#rune-0046}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `frontend` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the frontend obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `frontend` at rune 0046 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0047] gateway {Arch} {#rune-0047}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `gateway` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the gateway obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `gateway` at rune 0047 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0048] graph {#rune-0048}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `graph` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the graph obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `graph` at rune 0048 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0049] handler {#rune-0049}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `handler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the handler obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `handler` at rune 0049 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0050] hierarchy {Arch} {#rune-0050}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hierarchy` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hierarchy obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hierarchy` at rune 0050 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0051] hook {#rune-0051}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hook` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hook obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hook` at rune 0051 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0052] host {#rune-0052}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `host` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the host obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `host` at rune 0052 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0053] hub {#rune-0053}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hub` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the hub obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hub` at rune 0053 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0054] implementation {Arch} {#rune-0054}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `implementation` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the implementation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `implementation` at rune 0054 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0055] interface {Arch} {#rune-0055}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.

**Shadow:** tight coupling and type bleed.

**Pocket gloss:** A disciplined seam that lets unlike bodies cooperate without swapping internal organs.

### [0056] interpreter {Arch} {#rune-0056}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `interpreter` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the interpreter obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `interpreter` at rune 0056 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0057] kernel service {#rune-0057}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `kernel service` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the kernel service obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `kernel service` at rune 0057 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0058] layer {#rune-0058}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.

**Shadow:** misusing `layer` at rune 0058 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A stratum with a narrow job and limited allowed dependencies.

### [0059] library {#rune-0059}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `library` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the library obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `library` at rune 0059 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0060] listener {#rune-0060}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `listener` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the listener obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `listener` at rune 0060 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0061] machine boundary {#rune-0061}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `machine boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the machine boundary obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `machine boundary` at rune 0061 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0062] mediator {#rune-0062}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `mediator` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the mediator obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `mediator` at rune 0062 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0063] mesh {Arch} {#rune-0063}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `mesh` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the mesh obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `mesh` at rune 0063 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0064] microservice {#rune-0064}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `microservice` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the microservice obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `microservice` at rune 0064 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0065] middleware {#rune-0065}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.

**Shadow:** misusing `middleware` at rune 0065 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** Cross-cutting logic placed in the path between request and handler.

### [0066] module {Arch} {#rune-0066}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.

**Shadow:** misusing `module` at rune 0066 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A package of related definitions meant to travel as one thing.

### [0067] monolith {#rune-0067}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `monolith` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the monolith obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `monolith` at rune 0067 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0068] namespace {Arch} {#rune-0068}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `namespace` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the namespace obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `namespace` at rune 0068 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0069] node {Arch} {#rune-0069}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `node` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the node obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `node` at rune 0069 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0070] observer {#rune-0070}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `observer` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the observer obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `observer` at rune 0070 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0071] orchestrator {#rune-0071}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.

**Shadow:** misusing `orchestrator` at rune 0071 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A coordinator that sequences many services or tasks into one larger procedure.

### [0072] package {Arch} {#rune-0072}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `package` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the package obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `package` at rune 0072 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0073] pipeline {Arch} {#rune-0073}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `pipeline` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the pipeline obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `pipeline` at rune 0073 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0074] platform {#rune-0074}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `platform` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the platform obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `platform` at rune 0074 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0075] plugin {#rune-0075}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.

**Shadow:** misusing `plugin` at rune 0075 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** An extension point that lets capability be added without rewriting the host.

### [0076] port {Arch} {#rune-0076}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `port` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the port obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `port` at rune 0076 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0077] presenter {#rune-0077}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `presenter` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the presenter obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `presenter` at rune 0077 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0078] process manager {#rune-0078}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `process manager` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the process manager obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `process manager` at rune 0078 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0079] producer {#rune-0079}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `producer` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the producer obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `producer` at rune 0079 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0080] projection {#rune-0080}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `projection` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the projection obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `projection` at rune 0080 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0081] protocol boundary {#rune-0081}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `protocol boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the protocol boundary obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `protocol boundary` at rune 0081 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0082] proxy {Arch} {#rune-0082}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.

**Shadow:** misusing `proxy` at rune 0082 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A stand-in that speaks or receives on behalf of another body.

### [0083] queue {Arch} {#rune-0083}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `queue` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the queue obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `queue` at rune 0083 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0084] reactor {#rune-0084}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `reactor` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the reactor obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `reactor` at rune 0084 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0085] receiver {#rune-0085}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `receiver` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the receiver obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `receiver` at rune 0085 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0086] registry {#rune-0086}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `registry` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the registry obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `registry` at rune 0086 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0087] repository {Arch} {#rune-0087}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `repository` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the repository obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `repository` at rune 0087 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0088] resource boundary {#rune-0088}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `resource boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the resource boundary obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `resource boundary` at rune 0088 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0089] router {Arch} {#rune-0089}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `router` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the router obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `router` at rune 0089 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0090] scheduler {#rune-0090}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `scheduler` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the scheduler obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `scheduler` at rune 0090 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0091] seam {#rune-0091}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `seam` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the seam obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `seam` at rune 0091 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0092] service {#rune-0092}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.

**Shadow:** misusing `service` at rune 0092 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

**Pocket gloss:** A stable capability exposed for repeated use by others.

### [0093] service mesh {Arch} {#rune-0093}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `service mesh` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the service mesh obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `service mesh` at rune 0093 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0094] session boundary {#rune-0094}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `session boundary` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the session boundary obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `session boundary` at rune 0094 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0095] shard {Arch} {#rune-0095}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `shard` in its Arch sense is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the shard obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `shard` at rune 0095 in the Arch lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0096] shim {#rune-0096}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `shim` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the shim obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `shim` at rune 0096 in the architecture lane can let ownership can blur across layers; verify by requiring the caster to name the owning artifact and the caller-visible contract.

### [0097] singleton {#rune-0097}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `singleton` is a architecture and design rune for separate responsibility, boundary, and interface decisions; use it when the artifact needs the singleton obligation named before work proceeds.
